---
title: "05 — Random Forest Incivility Classifier"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "C:/Users/ammonsj/Ideas")
source("C:/Users/ammonsj/Ideas/_config.R")
source("C:/Users/ammonsj/Ideas/R/helpers.R")
```

## Check for existing model (fast-path for scoring only)

```{r check-model}
library(arrow)
library(data.table)
library(ranger)
library(pROC)
library(ggplot2)

model_path <- file.path(MODELS_DIR, "rf_incivility_model.rds")
cal_path   <- file.path(MODELS_DIR, "calibration_fn.rds")
fcols_path <- file.path(MODELS_DIR, "feature_cols.rds")

model_exists <- file.exists(model_path) && file.exists(cal_path) && file.exists(fcols_path)

if (model_exists) {
  message("Existing RF model found — skipping training, jumping to scoring.")
  rf_final     <- readRDS(model_path)
  calibrate_fn <- readRDS(cal_path)
  feature_cols <- readRDS(fcols_path)
  message("  Model loaded with ", length(feature_cols), " features.")
}
```

## Load feature matrix

```{r load, eval=!model_exists}
feature_df <- as.data.table(read_parquet(
  file.path(DATA_PANELS, "rf_features.parquet")
))
message("Loaded ", nrow(feature_df), " articles with ",
        ncol(feature_df), " columns")
```

## Create pseudo-labels (distant supervision)

```{r labels, eval=!model_exists}
# The uncivil_score distribution is extremely zero-inflated (~96% are 0).
# Using raw quantiles would make q50=q90=0, collapsing all labels.
# Instead, define labels based on zero vs non-zero structure:
#   Civil (0): articles with zero lexicon score (no incivility signal)
#   Uncivil (1): articles with score above the 75th percentile of non-zero scores
#   Excluded (NA): articles with low positive scores (ambiguous)

nonzero_scores <- feature_df$uncivil_score[feature_df$uncivil_score > 0 &
                                            !is.na(feature_df$uncivil_score)]
if (length(nonzero_scores) == 0) {
  stop("No articles have positive uncivil_score. Check lexicon scoring in step 03.")
}
q75_nonzero <- quantile(nonzero_scores, 0.75)

message("Score distribution: ",
        sum(feature_df$uncivil_score == 0), " zero, ",
        length(nonzero_scores), " nonzero")
message("Threshold for uncivil (p75 of nonzero): ", round(q75_nonzero, 3))

feature_df[, label := fifelse(
  uncivil_score >= q75_nonzero, 1L,
  fifelse(uncivil_score == 0, 0L, NA_integer_)
)]

labeled <- feature_df[!is.na(label)]
message("Labeled articles: ", nrow(labeled),
        " (incivil: ", sum(labeled$label == 1),
        ", civil: ", sum(labeled$label == 0), ")")

# Downsample civil class to at most 10x uncivil for balanced-ish training
n_uncivil <- sum(labeled$label == 1)
n_civil <- sum(labeled$label == 0)
if (n_civil > 10 * n_uncivil) {
  set.seed(SEED)
  civil_ids <- labeled[label == 0, which = TRUE]
  keep_civil <- sample(civil_ids, 10 * n_uncivil)
  uncivil_ids <- labeled[label == 1, which = TRUE]
  labeled <- labeled[sort(c(keep_civil, uncivil_ids))]
  message("Downsampled civil class: ", nrow(labeled),
          " (incivil: ", sum(labeled$label == 1),
          ", civil: ", sum(labeled$label == 0), ")")
}
```

## Grouped k-fold CV by newspaper

```{r cv, eval=!model_exists}
# Stratified grouped CV: assign newspapers to folds ensuring both classes
# in each fold. Classify each newspaper by its majority label, then
# stratify so each fold gets a mix of "mostly uncivil" and "mostly civil"
# newspapers.
set.seed(SEED)
np_class <- labeled[, .(frac_uncivil = mean(label)), by = newspaper_name]
np_class[, np_type := fifelse(frac_uncivil > 0.5, "uncivil", "civil")]

# Shuffle within each type, then assign folds round-robin
np_class <- np_class[sample(.N)]
np_class[, fold := rep(1:5, length.out = .N), by = np_type]

np_folds <- np_class[, .(newspaper_name, fold)]
labeled <- merge(labeled, np_folds, by = "newspaper_name")

# Feature columns (exclude identifiers and target)
id_cols <- c("article_id", "newspaper_name", "year", "year_month",
             "uncivil_score", "label", "fold")
feature_cols <- setdiff(names(labeled), id_cols)

# Remove any columns with all NA
na_cols <- feature_cols[sapply(labeled[, ..feature_cols], function(x) all(is.na(x)))]
if (length(na_cols) > 0) {
  message("Removing all-NA columns: ", paste(na_cols, collapse = ", "))
  feature_cols <- setdiff(feature_cols, na_cols)
}

# Replace remaining NAs with 0
for (col in feature_cols) {
  set(labeled, which(is.na(labeled[[col]])), col, 0)
}

message("Feature columns: ", length(feature_cols))
```

## Train and evaluate

```{r train, eval=!model_exists}
cv_results <- list()
all_preds <- data.table()

for (k in 1:5) {
  message("\n=== Fold ", k, " ===")
  train_idx <- labeled$fold != k
  test_idx <- labeled$fold == k

  train_data <- labeled[train_idx, c(feature_cols, "label"), with = FALSE]
  test_data <- labeled[test_idx, c(feature_cols, "label"), with = FALSE]

  # Convert label to factor with explicit levels for classification
  train_data[, label := factor(label, levels = c("0", "1"))]
  test_data_labels <- test_data$label  # integer 0/1
  test_data[, label := factor(label, levels = c("0", "1"))]

  message("  Train: ", nrow(train_data), " | Test: ", nrow(test_data))
  message("  Test class balance: 0=", sum(test_data_labels == 0),
          ", 1=", sum(test_data_labels == 1))

  # Train ranger
  rf <- ranger(
    label ~ .,
    data = train_data,
    num.trees = 500,
    mtry = floor(sqrt(length(feature_cols))),
    min.node.size = 10,
    probability = TRUE,
    importance = "impurity",
    seed = SEED,
    num.threads = max(2L, parallel::detectCores() - 4L)  # use most cores for training
  )

  # Predict on test
  pred <- predict(rf, test_data)
  p_incivil <- pred$predictions[, "1"]

  # Metrics (handle degenerate folds gracefully)
  auc_val <- tryCatch({
    roc_obj <- roc(factor(test_data_labels, levels = c(0, 1)),
                   p_incivil, quiet = TRUE)
    as.numeric(auc(roc_obj))
  }, error = function(e) {
    message("  AUC unavailable (single-class fold): ", e$message)
    NA_real_
  })

  # Brier score
  brier <- mean((p_incivil - test_data_labels)^2)

  cv_results[[k]] <- data.table(fold = k, auc = auc_val, brier = brier)
  message("  AUC: ", round(auc_val, 4), " | Brier: ", round(brier, 4))

  # Store predictions
  fold_preds <- data.table(
    article_id = labeled[test_idx, article_id],
    actual = test_data_labels,
    p_incivil = p_incivil,
    fold = k
  )
  all_preds <- rbind(all_preds, fold_preds)

  rm(rf, pred, train_data, test_data)
  gc()
}

cv_summary <- rbindlist(cv_results)

# Warn about degenerate folds instead of silently dropping
n_na_folds <- sum(is.na(cv_summary$auc))
if (n_na_folds > 0) {
  warning("CV: ", n_na_folds, " of 5 folds had NA AUC (single-class fold). ",
          "Check stratification. Reporting mean over ", 5 - n_na_folds, " valid folds.")
}

message("\n=== CV Summary ===")
message("Mean AUC: ", round(mean(cv_summary$auc, na.rm = TRUE), 4),
        " (SD: ", round(sd(cv_summary$auc, na.rm = TRUE), 4), ")",
        if (n_na_folds > 0) paste0(" [WARNING: ", n_na_folds, " NA folds excluded]") else "")
message("Mean Brier: ", round(mean(cv_summary$brier, na.rm = TRUE), 4))
```

## Probability calibration

```{r calibrate, eval=!model_exists}
# Fit isotonic calibration on all CV predictions
calibrate_fn <- fit_isotonic(all_preds$p_incivil, all_preds$actual)
all_preds[, p_calibrated := calibrate_fn(p_incivil)]

# Calibration plot
cal_data <- all_preds[, .(
  pred_bin = cut(p_calibrated, breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.1), include.lowest = TRUE)
)]
cal_data$predicted <- all_preds$p_calibrated
cal_data$actual <- all_preds$actual

cal_summary <- cal_data[, .(
  mean_pred = mean(predicted),
  mean_actual = mean(actual),
  n = .N
), by = pred_bin]

p_cal <- ggplot(cal_summary, aes(x = mean_pred, y = mean_actual)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = n)) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  labs(x = "Mean Predicted Probability", y = "Observed Frequency",
       title = "RF Calibration Plot") +
  theme_minimal() +
  coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1))

save_figure(p_cal, "rf_calibration", width = 6, height = 6)
print(p_cal)
```

## Train final model on all labeled data (or load existing)

```{r final-model, eval=!model_exists}
model_path <- file.path(MODELS_DIR, "rf_incivility_model.rds")
cal_path   <- file.path(MODELS_DIR, "calibration_fn.rds")
fcols_path <- file.path(MODELS_DIR, "feature_cols.rds")

if (file.exists(model_path) && file.exists(cal_path) && file.exists(fcols_path)) {
  message("Loading existing RF model (skipping retraining).")
  rf_final     <- readRDS(model_path)
  calibrate_fn <- readRDS(cal_path)
  feature_cols <- readRDS(fcols_path)
  message("  Model loaded with ", length(feature_cols), " features.")
} else {
  message("Training final RF model on all labeled data...")
  train_all <- labeled[, c(feature_cols, "label"), with = FALSE]
  train_all[, label := factor(label, levels = c("0", "1"))]

  rf_final <- ranger(
    label ~ .,
    data = train_all,
    num.trees = 500,
    mtry = floor(sqrt(length(feature_cols))),
    min.node.size = 10,
    probability = TRUE,
    importance = "impurity",
    seed = SEED,
    num.threads = max(2L, parallel::detectCores() - 4L)  # use most cores for final model
  )

  # Save model
  saveRDS(rf_final, model_path)
  saveRDS(calibrate_fn, cal_path)
  saveRDS(feature_cols, fcols_path)
  message("Saved final RF model and calibration function.")
}
```

## Variable importance

```{r importance}
# Load model if not already in memory (e.g., when model_exists was TRUE)
if (!exists("rf_final")) {
  rf_final <- readRDS(file.path(MODELS_DIR, "rf_incivility_model.rds"))
}

imp <- data.table(
  feature = names(rf_final$variable.importance),
  importance = rf_final$variable.importance
)
imp <- imp[order(-importance)][1:20]

# Hand-curated English labels based on top-loading words per SVD component
# (see inspect_svd.R for derivation)
label_map <- c(
  "n_words"     = "Article Length (word count)",
  "ocr_quality" = "OCR Quality Score",
  "svd_1"  = "Formal Political Speech",
  "svd_2"  = "Social Titles & Personal News",
  "svd_3"  = "Abbreviations & OCR Artifacts",
  "svd_4"  = "Non-English Text (German)",
  "svd_5"  = "Legislative Language",
  "svd_6"  = "Non-English Text (Spanish)",
  "svd_11" = "Local Government & Civic",
  "svd_12" = "Court & Legal Reporting",
  "svd_24" = "Financial & Real Estate",
  "svd_26" = "Banking & Commerce",
  "svd_30" = "Legal Cases & Religious",
  "svd_32" = "Monetary Reporting",
  "svd_45" = "Measurements & Classifieds",
  "svd_47" = "Property & Tax Listings",
  "svd_54" = "Government Agencies",
  "svd_58" = "Political Districts & Companies",
  "svd_71" = "Political Parties & Legislation",
  "svd_93" = "City & Place Names"
)
imp[, label := ifelse(feature %in% names(label_map),
                       label_map[feature], feature)]

p_imp <- ggplot(imp, aes(x = reorder(label, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = "Importance (Impurity)",
       title = "Top 20 Features \u2014 RF Incivility Classifier") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_text(size = 10))

save_figure(p_imp, "rf_variable_importance", width = 10, height = 7)
print(p_imp)

gc()
```

## Score full corpus

```{r score-corpus}
# PARALLEL SCORING OPTION:
# If USE_PARALLEL_SCORING is TRUE, this chunk only verifies model artifacts
# and prints instructions for launching parallel workers. The inline loop
# is skipped. Set to FALSE to score inline (slower, single-threaded).
USE_PARALLEL_SCORING <- TRUE

library(quanteda)

# Load SVD model info for transforming new articles
svd_info <- readRDS(file.path(MODELS_DIR, "svd_model_info.rds"))

# Verify that IDF weights are present (added in fixed step 04)
if (is.null(svd_info$idf_weights)) {
  stop("svd_model_info.rds does not contain idf_weights. ",
       "Re-run step 04 (feature engineering) first.")
}
message("Loaded SVD model with ", length(svd_info$vocab), " vocab terms, ",
        length(svd_info$idf_weights), " IDF weights, ",
        svd_info$n_components, " SVD components")

# Minimum word count: articles shorter than this get NA (lexicon fallback)
MIN_WORDS_FOR_RF <- 10L

scored_dir <- file.path(DATA_PARQUET, "articles_scored")
scored_files <- list.files(scored_dir, pattern = "\\.parquet$", full.names = TRUE)

rf_scored_dir <- file.path(DATA_PANELS, "rf_scored")
dir.create(rf_scored_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

# Check how many years still need scoring
n_already_done <- length(list.files(rf_scored_dir, pattern = "\\.parquet$"))
n_todo <- length(scored_files) - n_already_done
message("RF scoring: ", n_already_done, " years done, ", n_todo, " remaining of ", length(scored_files))

if (USE_PARALLEL_SCORING && n_todo > 0) {
  message("\n============================================================")
  message("PARALLEL SCORING MODE ENABLED")
  message("============================================================")
  message("Model artifacts are ready. Skipping inline scoring loop.")
  message("Launch parallel workers with:")
  message("  powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File R\\_launch_parallel_scoring.ps1")
  message("")
  message("After all workers finish, resume pipeline from step 7:")
  message("  Rscript run_pipeline.R 7 10")
  message("============================================================")

  # Write a sentinel file so we know scoring is pending
  writeLines(as.character(Sys.time()),
             file.path(MODELS_DIR, ".parallel_scoring_pending"))

  # CRITICAL: Stop the pipeline here. Step 06 expects rf_scored files.
  # If we don't stop, the pipeline will immediately try to build treatment
  # panels from non-existent scored data.
  stop("PARALLEL SCORING: Pipeline halted after model training. ",
       "Run parallel workers, then resume with: Rscript run_pipeline.R 7 10",
       call. = FALSE)
} else if (n_todo == 0) {
  message("All years already RF-scored. Nothing to do.")
}

# Only run inline loop when NOT in parallel mode (or everything is already done)
if (!USE_PARALLEL_SCORING || n_todo == 0) {

for (sf in scored_files) {
  yr <- gsub(".*scored_(\\d{4})\\.parquet", "\\1", sf)
  outpath <- file.path(rf_scored_dir, paste0("rf_scored_", yr, ".parquet"))
  tmppath <- paste0(outpath, ".tmp")  # atomic write: write to tmp, then rename

  if (file.exists(outpath)) {
    message("Year ", yr, " already RF-scored, skipping.")
    next
  }

  message("RF-scoring year ", yr, "...")
  dt_year <- as.data.table(read_parquet(sf))

  # Always store lexicon-based score (properly scaled to [0,1] via min-max)
  # uncivil_score is rate per 1000 words; convert to crude probability
  dt_year[, p_incivil_lexicon := {
    s <- uncivil_score
    s_pos <- s[s > 0 & !is.na(s)]
    s_max <- if (length(s_pos) > 0) quantile(s_pos, 0.99) else 1
    if (is.na(s_max) || s_max == 0) s_max <- 1  # safe default
    pmin(1, pmax(0, s / s_max))
  }]
  dt_year[, p_incivil := NA_real_]

  # Track fallback count for logging
  n_fallback <- 0L

  # Process in chunks to avoid memory blow-up on large years
  chunk_size <- 50000L
  n_total <- nrow(dt_year)
  n_chunks <- ceiling(n_total / chunk_size)
  message("  Articles: ", n_total, " -> ", n_chunks, " chunks")

  for (ci in seq_len(n_chunks)) {
    row_start <- (ci - 1L) * chunk_size + 1L
    row_end <- min(ci * chunk_size, n_total)
    chunk_rows <- row_start:row_end

    tryCatch({
      chunk_text <- dt_year$article[chunk_rows]

      # --- Handle short/empty articles ---
      # Articles with fewer than MIN_WORDS_FOR_RF words after preprocessing
      # get degenerate SVD features. Flag them for lexicon fallback.
      word_counts <- stringi::stri_count_words(chunk_text)
      word_counts[is.na(word_counts)] <- 0L
      short_mask <- word_counts < MIN_WORDS_FOR_RF

      corp_c <- corpus(chunk_text)
      toks_c <- tokens(corp_c, remove_punct = TRUE, remove_numbers = TRUE,
                       remove_symbols = TRUE) |>
        tokens_tolower() |>
        tokens_remove(stopwords("en")) |>
        tokens_wordstem()

      # --- FIXED: Correct order of operations ---
      # 1. Build raw DFM
      # 2. Match to training vocabulary FIRST (trim to same features)
      # 3. Apply saved IDF weights (not recomputed per-chunk)
      dfmat_c <- dfm(toks_c)
      dfmat_c <- dfm_match(dfmat_c, svd_info$vocab)  # FIRST: match to training vocab

      # Apply saved training IDF weights via sparse matrix operations (memory efficient)
      idf_vec <- svd_info$idf_weights[svd_info$vocab]
      sp_mat <- Matrix::as(dfmat_c, "dgCMatrix")
      sp_mat <- sp_mat %*% Matrix::Diagonal(x = idf_vec)  # TF * saved_IDF (stays sparse)

      # Apply SVD projection: (X - center) %*% rotation
      # Use centering trick to avoid densifying: (X-c)R = XR - cR
      if (!is.null(svd_info$center)) {
        center_proj <- as.numeric(crossprod(svd_info$center, svd_info$rotation))
        svd_raw <- as.matrix(sp_mat %*% svd_info$rotation)
        svd_scores <- sweep(svd_raw, 2, center_proj, `-`)
        rm(svd_raw)
      } else {
        svd_scores <- as.matrix(sp_mat %*% svd_info$rotation)
      }
      rm(sp_mat)

      svd_dt <- as.data.table(svd_scores)
      names(svd_dt) <- paste0("svd_", seq_len(ncol(svd_dt)))

      # --- Metadata features (sub-scores EXCLUDED to match training) ---
      meta_c <- dt_year[chunk_rows, .(front_page, headline_length, n_words,
                                       has_byline, ocr_quality, page_num)]
      # Guard against NAs in metadata
      meta_c[is.na(headline_length), headline_length := 0L]
      meta_c[is.na(has_byline), has_byline := 0L]
      meta_c[is.na(front_page), front_page := 0L]
      meta_c[is.na(ocr_quality), ocr_quality := 0]
      meta_c[is.na(page_num), page_num := 0L]
      meta_c[is.na(n_words), n_words := 0L]

      feat_c <- cbind(meta_c, svd_dt)

      for (fc in feature_cols) {
        if (!fc %in% names(feat_c)) feat_c[, (fc) := 0]
      }
      feat_c <- feat_c[, ..feature_cols]
      for (col in feature_cols) {
        set(feat_c, which(is.na(feat_c[[col]])), col, 0)
      }

      pred_c <- predict(rf_final, feat_c)
      p_vals <- calibrate_fn(pred_c$predictions[, "1"])

      # Set short articles to NA (will get fallback below)
      p_vals[short_mask] <- NA_real_
      n_fallback <- n_fallback + sum(short_mask)

      set(dt_year, chunk_rows, "p_incivil", p_vals)

      rm(corp_c, toks_c, dfmat_c, mat_c, svd_scores, svd_dt,
         meta_c, feat_c, pred_c, p_vals, chunk_text, word_counts, short_mask)
      gc()
    }, error = function(e) {
      message("  Chunk ", ci, " failed: ", e$message, " -> lexicon fallback")
      n_fallback <<- n_fallback + length(chunk_rows)
      # Fallback: use SCALED lexicon score (not raw rate per 1000 words)
      set(dt_year, chunk_rows, "p_incivil",
          dt_year$p_incivil_lexicon[chunk_rows])
    })
  }

  # Fill remaining NAs with scaled lexicon fallback (not raw uncivil_score)
  # (n_fallback already includes short articles from chunk loop — don't double-count)
  na_remaining <- sum(is.na(dt_year$p_incivil))
  if (na_remaining > 0) {
    dt_year[is.na(p_incivil), p_incivil := p_incivil_lexicon]
  }

  if (n_fallback > 0) {
    message("  WARNING: ", n_fallback, " / ", n_total,
            " articles used lexicon fallback (",
            round(n_fallback / n_total * 100, 1), "%)")
  }

  # Atomic write: write to tmp file, then rename
  write_parquet(
    dt_year[, .(article_id, newspaper_name, year, year_month, date,
                front_page, n_words, page_num, ocr_quality,
                uncivil_score, p_incivil, p_incivil_lexicon)],
    tmppath
  )
  file.rename(tmppath, outpath)
  message("  Saved ", nrow(dt_year), " RF-scored articles for year ", yr)

  rm(dt_year)
  gc()
}

message("\nFull corpus scoring complete.")

} # end if (!USE_PARALLEL_SCORING || n_todo == 0)
```

## Save CV results table

```{r save-cv-table, eval=!model_exists}
library(modelsummary)

cv_summary[, fold := as.character(fold)]
overall <- data.table(fold = "Mean", auc = mean(cv_summary$auc, na.rm = TRUE),
                      brier = mean(cv_summary$brier, na.rm = TRUE))
cv_table <- rbind(cv_summary, overall)
cv_table[, auc := round(auc, 4)]
cv_table[, brier := round(brier, 4)]

# Save as LaTeX
tex <- knitr::kable(cv_table, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE,
                    caption = "Random Forest Cross-Validation Performance",
                    col.names = c("Fold", "AUC-ROC", "Brier Score"))
save_table(tex, "rf_cv_performance")
message("Saved RF CV performance table.")
```
